purpose: Define repeatable document-selection rules for building investor-advisor reference slices across Korean corporate groups.

scopePrinciples.name: reference-slice, not full archive by default
scopePrinciples.rule: A group corpus may be scoped to the documents needed for a traceable demo, paper evaluation, or commercial use case. If it is not a full archive, the scope must be stated explicitly.

scopePrinciples.name: claim-driven collection
scopePrinciples.rule: Additional official materials are collected only when a claim, evaluation scenario, or runtime feature needs them.

scopePrinciples.name: same template across groups
scopePrinciples.rule: Each target group must use the same inventory, provenance, selection-rationale, extraction, claim-linking, wiki, and validation steps before being marked paper-ready.

scopePrinciples.name: common output schema after source intake
scopePrinciples.rule: Group-specific intake adapters may normalize different raw source layouts, but the emitted source inventory, extraction report, narrative claim queue, source-backed claim manifest, wiki namespace, and evaluation scenario schemas must remain common across groups.

selectionRules.name: official issuer provenance
selectionRules.rule: Prefer official issuer IR, governance, financial, shareholder-return, and disclosure materials with a stable public source page, public document URL, or public filing URL. A source-page URL is acceptable when document downloads are dynamic or session-based, as long as document title, period, local checksum, access date, and extraction hash are recorded.

selectionRules.name: regulatory filing provenance
selectionRules.rule: Periodic-report and material-disclosure claims should be linked to DART receipt numbers or public filing URLs when available.

selectionRules.name: audit baseline
selectionRules.rule: Audit reports and consolidated audit reports are retained when they anchor accounting-grounded financial history or verify stale/hallucination-prone financial claims.

selectionRules.name: current periodic disclosure
selectionRules.rule: Recent annual, semiannual, and quarterly reports are retained when they support revenue, operating income, risk, segment, investment, or business-update claims.

selectionRules.name: current earnings and IR material
selectionRules.rule: Recent earnings decks and official IR presentations are retained when they support investor briefings, headline metrics, and segment-performance explanations.

selectionRules.name: shareholder-value and capital actions
selectionRules.rule: Value-up plans, shareholder-return materials, buybacks, preferred-share actions, capital actions, and related official notices are retained as high-priority investor-advisor sources.

selectionRules.name: governance baseline
selectionRules.rule: Articles of incorporation, board/governance policy, disclosure policy, and shareholder-rights materials are retained when they affect capital allocation, governance interpretation, or investor rights.

selectionRules.name: third-party market view as secondary context
selectionRules.rule: Analyst reports and broker materials may be retained as secondary market-view context, but facts must be checked against official issuer, DART, KRX, or reputable public-news sources.

selectionRules.name: rights and redistribution boundary
selectionRules.rule: Sources without clear redistribution rights must remain manifest-only or metadata-only. Full text should not be shipped to Replit or published unless rights are verified.

selectionRules.name: machine extraction readiness
selectionRules.rule: A source can be promoted to runtime wiki context only after extraction or manual transcription is checked, hashed, and linked to source-level provenance. Exact document URLs are preferred but not required when an official IR source page and immutable local checksum identify the document.

selectionRules.name: claim-level traceability
selectionRules.rule: No source is sufficient by itself for runtime answers. Each answerable claim must point to a source, source kind, date/period, companyId, companyScope, and verification state.

selectionRules.name: company-scoped claim routing
selectionRules.rule: Every runtime-promoted claim must include companyId. Group or issuer-level claims may use the listed issuer companyId, while affiliate-specific claims must use the affiliate companyId recorded in configs/groups.json.

paperUseLevels.primary-source-candidate: Official or regulatory source that can support paper and runtime claims after claim-level linking.
paperUseLevels.primary-source-candidate-after-type-chec: Official source requiring media-type or extraction verification before claim promotion.
paperUseLevels.secondary-context-only: External interpretation source for market-view comparison, not primary factual support.
paperUseLevels.hold-until-reviewed: Present in the corpus but not usable until provenance, rights, and selection role are reviewed.
